title: 2026 JAMB Direct Entry Registration Deadline Announced
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: Candidates seeking admission through Direct Entry are hereby informed that the 2026 registration exercise, conducted by Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board, will close on Saturday, April 25, 2026. Registration, which commenced on March 2, 2026, is strictly carried out at approved JAMB offices and accredited CBT centres nationwide. Applicants are advised to complete their registration early and ensure all required documents are available to avoid disqualification.
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially fixed Saturday, April 25, 2026, as the deadline for the 2026 Direct Entry (DE) registration. 

All prospective Direct Entry candidates are required to register only at JAMB State Offices, Zonal Offices, and accredited CBT centres nationwide. The application form for the 2026 DE registration costs â¦5,700.

Applicants must ensure they complete their registration within the stipulated period, as late registration will not be entertained.

title: UNILORIN ANNOUNCES APPOINTMENT OF 43 NEW PROFESSORS ACROSS FACULTIES
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: The University of Ilorin has announced the promotion of 43 academic staff members to the rank of Professor, marking a significant milestone in the institutionÃ¢ÂÂs commitment to academic excellence and research development.
fullContent:
The University of Ilorin has announced the promotion of 43 academic staff members to the prestigious rank of Professor, underscoring the institutionÃ¢ÂÂs continued dedication to academic excellence, quality teaching, and impactful research.

The announcement, which was made by the university management, reflects the institutionÃ¢ÂÂs rigorous standards for career advancement and its commitment to recognizing outstanding contributions in teaching, research, and community service. The newly promoted professors cut across various faculties and disciplines, highlighting the universityÃ¢ÂÂs broad-based investment in intellectual growth and development.

This development is widely regarded as a significant milestone for the university, as it not only strengthens its academic workforce but also enhances its capacity for high-level research, innovation, and postgraduate supervision. The elevation of these scholars is expected to further boost the institutionÃ¢ÂÂs reputation both nationally and internationally.

University authorities emphasized that the promotions followed a thorough and merit-based evaluation process, in line with established academic criteria. The exercise also serves as motivation for other members of staff to strive for excellence in their respective fields.

The promotion of the 43 academic staff members is a clear indication of the University of IlorinÃ¢ÂÂs resolve to maintain its status as one of NigeriaÃ¢ÂÂs leading universities, while continuously contributing to knowledge production and national development.

title: NELFUND Debunks Misinformation on Student Upkeep Allowance
summary: The Nigeria Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) has clarified that the approved monthly upkeep allowance for students remains N20,000, debunking claims of an increase to N25,000 or N50,000. Students are advised to ignore misinformation and rely only on official updates from NELFUND.
fullContent:
The Nigeria Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) has addressed recent misinformation circulating among students regarding the monthly upkeep allowance provided under its scheme.

In an official clarification, the Fund stated that the approved monthly upkeep allowance remains N20,000, contrary to widespread reports suggesting an increase to N25,000 or N50,000.

NELFUND urged students and the general public to disregard such misleading claims, emphasizing that no changes have been made to the current allowance structure.

Students are further advised to rely only on verified information from official NELFUND channels and trusted sources to avoid confusion and stay properly informed.

title: JAMB Reopens UTME Subject Combination Correction for 2026
summary: JAMB has reopened the portal for correcting UTME subject combinations for the 2026 exam. Affected candidates are advised to visit accredited CBT centres with required documents to make corrections promptly. The board warns that the window may close at any time, urging immediate action.
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially reopened its portal for the correction of UTME subject combinations ahead of the 2026 examination.

This development provides an opportunity for candidates who selected incorrect subject combinations during registration to make necessary adjustments and avoid jeopardizing their admission prospects. Affected candidates are advised to act promptly by visiting any accredited JAMB CBT centre with their registration slip and a valid means of identification to request a correction of data.

JAMB also emphasized that the correction window may close at short notice, urging candidates to take immediate action.

title: Campus Update: New Regulations on Student Conduct
summary: Management of several Nigerian tertiary institutions has released fresh circulars regarding student dressing and campus conduct to maintain professional standards.
fullContent:
Several Nigerian tertiary institution managements have released fresh circulars regarding student dressing and campus conduct for the current academic session.

The new regulations aim to maintain professional standards within campus environments across universities, polytechnics, and colleges of education nationwide.

Key highlights of the regulations include:
- Students must wear appropriate attire that reflects academic decorum
- Sagging of trousers and indecent dressing are strictly prohibited
- Students must carry valid ID cards at all times on campus
- Noise-making and disruptive behaviour in lecture halls will attract disciplinary action

Students are advised to visit their departmental notice boards for full details on approved dress codes and conduct rules specific to their institution. Defaulters may face suspension or other disciplinary measures as outlined in their institution's student handbook.

Big Quams Media will continue to update students on important institutional announcements as they are released.

title: BIG QUAMS MEDIA NIMC SOLUTION HUB  (Fast & Reliable Solutions for All Your NIN Needs)
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: BIG QUAMS MEDIA offers independent assistance for NIN-related services, helping individuals resolve issues quickly and conveniently. All services are handled efficiently to ensure a smooth and stress-free experience.
fullContent:
BIG QUAMS MEDIA presents its NIMC Solution Hub, offering fast and reliable assistance for all your National Identification Number (NIN) needs. Please note, these services are independently handled by BIG QUAMS MEDIA to help individuals easily process and resolve NIN-related issues without stress.

From corrections to retrieval and validation, BIG QUAMS MEDIA ensures a smooth, efficient, and hassle-free experience. Our goal is to provide quick solutions while maintaining accuracy and professionalism, saving you time and effort.

Key Services:
 Plastic ID Card processing
 Digital NIN Slips
 NIN Modifications (Correction of details)
 NIN Retrieval
 NIN Validation
IPE Clearance

title: Top 10 High-Demand Courses for 2026 Admissions
summary: Choosing the right course is as important as choosing the right school. Our latest analysis highlights high-demand fields like Data Science, Nursing, and Education-Mathematics.
fullContent:

title: Scholarship Alert: Federal Government BEA Awards 2026/2027
summary: The Federal Ministry of Education has invited Nigerians to participate in the 2026/2027 BEA Scholarship Awards covering undergraduate and postgraduate studies in various countries.
fullContent:
The Honourable Minister of Education has invited interested Nigerians to participate in the 2026/2027 Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards.

This prestigious scholarship covers both undergraduate and postgraduate studies in partner countries including Russia, China, Hungary, Cuba, Romania, Morocco, Serbia, and others.

Eligibility requirements:
- Must be a Nigerian citizen with a valid passport
- Minimum of 5 credits in WAEC/NECO including English and Mathematics
- Must not be older than 25 years (undergraduate) or 35 years (postgraduate)
- Must have completed or be completing their current level of study

How to apply:
1. Visit the Federal Ministry of Education website for the application portal
2. Complete the online application form with accurate information
3. Upload required documents: credentials, passport, birth certificate, medical report
4. Submit before the closing date

Big Quams Media offers full application support including document preparation, personal statement writing, and submission guidance. Contact us on WhatsApp to get started with your BEA scholarship application.

title: Effective Study Habits for Post-UTME Excellence
summary: Success in Post-UTME requires strategy. Learn how to use past questions effectively, manage time during CBT exams, and master formats used by top universities.
fullContent:

Here are proven techniques to help you perform at your best:

1. Study Past Questions
Get the past questions specific to your institution and course. Most schools repeat question patterns. Focus on the last 5ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ10 years of past questions.

2. Understand the Exam Format
Different schools have different formats. Some test English only, others include subject-specific questions. Know exactly what to expect before exam day.

3. Time Management
In CBT exams, timing is critical. Practice answering 60 questions in 45 minutes to build speed. Never spend more than 1 minute on a single question.

4. Eliminate Wrong Answers
Use the process of elimination. Even if you are unsure, crossing out obvious wrong options improves your chance of selecting the correct answer.

5. Revision Strategy
Do not read everything ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ focus on areas with the highest frequency in past questions. Spend 70% of your time on your weak subjects.

6. Rest Before the Exam
Avoiding sleep the night before an exam reduces cognitive performance. Aim for at least 7 hours of sleep.

Big Quams Media offers personalised Post-UTME coaching sessions. Chat with us on WhatsApp to begin your preparation today.

title: Understanding CAPS Admission Status Meanings
summary: What does 'Admission in Progress' or 'Recommended' mean on your JAMB CAPS? We break down every status so you always know where you stand.
fullContent:
The JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) uses specific status labels that many students find confusing. Here is what each status means:

Admission in Progress
Your institution has found your details in the system and is processing your admission. No action is needed ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ wait for the status to change.

Recommended
Your institution has recommended you for admission and submitted your details to JAMB. You are close to being admitted but must wait for JAMB's final approval.

Withdrawn
The institution has withdrawn their admission offer. Contact your institution's admissions office immediately for clarification.

Important reminders:
- Always log into CAPS using your JAMB profile email and password
- Accept your admission within 3 days of it showing as 'Admitted'
- Do not use your registration number as your CAPS login ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ use your email

title: Mental Health and the Nigerian Student
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: The academic journey can be stressful. We discuss practical ways to maintain mental well-being while pursuing your degree at any Nigerian institution.
fullContent:
The academic journey in Nigerian institutions can be very demanding, as students often have to balance lectures, assignments, financial pressures, and personal responsibilities all at once. While striving to achieve academic success and reach your goals, it is important to remember that your mental and physical well-being should never be neglected. Many students experience stress, anxiety, and burnout but choose to ignore these feelings, which can eventually affect both their performance and overall quality of life. Learning to manage your time properly by creating a realistic study schedule and avoiding last-minute cramming can reduce unnecessary pressure, while taking short breaks helps to refresh your mind and improve concentration. It is also important to talk to someone you trust, such as a friend, family member, or mentor, because sharing your thoughts can ease emotional stress and make challenges feel more manageable.

In addition to mental health, physical well-being plays a major role in academic success. Your body needs proper care to function effectively, so maintaining healthy habits like eating balanced meals, staying hydrated, exercising regularly, and getting enough sleep is essential. Even simple activities like walking can boost your energy and improve your mood, while proper rest helps your brain stay sharp and focused. Students should also learn to study smarter by using effective techniques such as group discussions, practicing past questions, and breaking down large tasks into smaller, achievable goals. It is perfectly okay to seek help when needed, whether from lecturers or fellow students, as no one is expected to figure everything out alone.

Building a strong support system with positive and encouraging people can also make a significant difference in your academic journey. Being around individuals who motivate and understand you can help reduce stress and keep you focused on your goals. At the same time, it is important to avoid unhealthy comparisons with others, as everyoneÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs journey is different and progress happens at different paces. Whenever you start to feel overwhelmed, do not ignore the signs; instead, take a moment to pause, breathe, and reset. Remember that success is not only measured by grades or certificates but also by your ability to stay healthy, balanced, and resilient throughout the process. Taking care of yourself is not a distraction from your goalsÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂit is a necessary part of achieving them.

title: JAMB Warns Candidates Against Skipping Mock-UTME
summary: JAMB has warned candidates who selected the Mock-UTME during registration that participation is now mandatory. The mock exam was scheduled for March 28, 2026.
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has issued a stern warning to candidates who selected the Mock-UTME option during their 2026 registration.

According to JAMB, participation in the Mock-UTME for those who selected it is now mandatory. The board has emphasised that failure to appear for the mock examination without valid reason may affect a candidate's standing in the main UTME exercise.

Key details about the Mock-UTME:
- It was scheduled for Saturday, March 28, 2026
- The mock exam is conducted at designated CBT centres nationwide
- Candidates should print their mock examination slip from the JAMB portal
- The exercise covers the same subjects and format as the main UTME
- Results of the mock exam are used purely for preparation and practice

Benefits of the Mock-UTME:
The mock examination is designed to familiarise candidates with the CBT environment, reducing exam anxiety and improving performance in the main examination. Candidates who participate consistently score higher in the actual UTME.

Candidates who face challenges printing their mock slip or locating their centre should contact Big Quams Media immediately for assistance.
